Ranunculus acris, also known as Meadow Buttercup, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. This plant is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus acris include isothiocyanates, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ranunculus acris include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat urinary tract issues and other conditions.
